name: What proof do I need to win a claim for chemical inhalation at work?

mainEntity.name: What proof do I need to win a claim for chemical inhalation at work?

acceptedAnswer.text: Medical link to work exposure, safety sheets, witnesses, air tests[1][4].
acceptedAnswer.description: Medical records linking your condition to specific workplace exposures, plus job descriptions, safety data sheets, and witness accounts of hazards. OSHA requires evidence like air sampling showing overexposure potential[1][3][4]. Doctor opinions via QME tie illness to job. - Exposure history: duration, substances, lack of PPE. - No pre-work lung issues in records. Collect coworker statements, photos of conditions, and file OSHA complaint for support docs.

description: Medical link to work exposure, safety sheets, witnesses, air tests[1][4].
